summary: Parallel review: FAIL (5 critical, 2 warning, 7 suggestion). Compound gate: WARN

findingsList.text: - `bin/lib/flows.mjs:188` — multi-review merged output labels 🔴 simplicity as `[simplicity veto]`, 🟡/🔵 as `[simplicity]`.

Implementation matches the requirement exactly: Simplicity 🔴 findings are tagged `[simplicity veto]` in combined output for both flows (build-verify via new `tagSimplicityFinding()` helper in run.mjs:1290-1297; full-stack via pre-existing `mergeReviewFindings` at flows.mjs:188). The 4 new tests include an explicit cross-flow contract assertion, and all 53 flows tests pass. Scope is tight and purely additive.
🟡 bin/lib/flows.mjs:188 — `mergeReviewFindings` duplicates the emoji-preserving tag logic inline instead of delegating to the new `tagSimplicityFinding`; two copies of the same rule will drift.

🔴 bin/lib/flows.mjs:210 — Premature abstraction: `tagSimplicityFinding()` has exactly 1 production caller (`bin/lib/run.mjs:1292`) while near-identical emoji-preserving tag logic lives inline in `mergeReviewFindings()` at `flows.mjs:186-191`. Fix by refactoring `mergeReviewFindings` to call `tagSimplicityFinding` for its simplicity-critical row (→ 2 call sites, removes duplication), or inline the 7-line helper at its single use site.

Evidence: `grep` confirms 1 definition + 1 import + 1 call; `mergeReviewFindings` at flows.mjs:186-191 duplicates the `^([🔴🟡🔵])\s*/u` splice pattern; `npm test` → 596/596 pass; both flows do emit `[simplicity veto]` as claimed.
